skill_invocation: invokeMarketingSkill("emails", { subjectLine, customerContext, brandVoice, intent: "alternate phrasing", originalBody }, { variants: 2 })
brand_voice: Operator. Jack writes the email, not 'we'. No 'thank you for choosing us' — the deposit already cleared. Plain, concrete, no exclamation points. Signed '— Jack / Day14'.

customerContext.scenario: Sent at 5pm ET by the build agent when planned-vs-shipped delta exceeds one ticket. Customer trust hinges on Jack naming the miss without spin.
customerContext.topic: End-of-day update on a day where a ticket slipped. Name the slip, reassure on launch date (buffer is there for this).
constraints: Name the slip; don't hide it.

originalBody:
Slower day. {{blocker_short_description}} took longer than the estimate.

What's done: {{progress_made}}.
What slipped to tomorrow: {{slipped_item}}.

Still on track for launch {{launch_date}} — the buffer's there for exactly this.

Preview: {{preview_url}}.

— Jack
Day14

variants.body:
[needs-API-key] Variant 1 of eod-update-bad — tighter receipt-style rewrite of:

Slower day. {{blocker_short_description}} took longer than the estimate.

[…rest of original preserved verbatim — re-run with marketing-skills:emails wired to populate.]
variants.rationale: placeholder #1 — strategy: tight-receipt (one-line acknowledgement, numbered next steps)

variants.body:

Slower day. {{blocker_short_description}} took longer than the estimate.

[…rest of original preserved verbatim — re-run with marketing-skills:emails wired to populate.]
variants.rationale: placeholder #2 — strategy: warmer-ops (single sentence of context before the next step)
